name: Welcome to the Playground
overview: Orphaned sisters Vi and Powder bring trouble to Zaun's underground streets in the wake of a heist in posh Piltover.

name: Some Mysteries Are Better Left Unsolved
overview: Idealistic inventor Jayce attempts to harness magic through science — despite his mentor's warnings. Criminal kingpin Silco tests a powerful substance.

name: The Base Violence Necessary for Change
overview: An epic showdown between old rivals results in a fateful moment for Zaun. Jayce and Viktor risk it all for their research.

name: Happy Progress Day!
overview: With Piltover prospering from their tech, Jayce and Viktor weigh their next move. A familiar face re-emerges from Zaun to wreak havoc.

name: Everybody Wants to Be My Enemy
overview: Rogue enforcer Caitlyn tours the undercity to track down Silco. Jayce puts a target on his back trying to root out Piltover corruption.

name: When These Walls Come Tumbling Down
overview: An eager protégé undermines his mentor on the council as a magical tech rapidly evolves. With authorities in pursuit, Jinx must face her past.

name: The Boy Savior
overview: Caitlyn and Vi meet an ally in Zaun’s streets and head into a frenzied battle with a common foe. Viktor makes a dire decision.

name: Oil and Water
overview: Disowned heir Mel and her visiting mother trade combat tactics. Caitlyn and Vi forge an unlikely alliance. Jinx undergoes a startling change.

name: The Monster You Created
overview: Perilously close to war, the leaders of Piltover and Zaun reach an ultimatum. But a fateful standoff changes both cities forever.

name: Heavy Is the Crown
overview: Vi and Caitlyn wrestle with how best to respond in the wake of a terrible tragedy that claims lives — and escalates tensions between the twin cities.

name: Watch It All Burn
overview: With Piltover ready for war, the Undercity weighs its options. Jinx lies low and plots her next move. A pivotal conversation takes place.

name: Finally Got the Name Right
overview: Caitlyn doubles down on her hunt for Jinx. Ambessa accepts a fateful meeting. Changes in Zaun lead to a shocking discovery.

name: Paint the Town Blue
overview: As rumors of Jinx's return swirl, Ambessa pursues her target with renewed enthusiasm. Jinx and Sevika go undercover and into the belly of the beast.

name: Blisters and Bedrock
overview: Vi awakens to surprising news. An unsettling reunion isn't what it seems. Caitlyn uncovers the origins of Shimmer.

name: The Message Hidden Within the Pattern
overview: Healing comes from a familiar face — in an unfamiliar place. A stunning betrayal threatens to change countless lives.

name: Pretend Like It's the First Time
overview: A moment of darkness, a moment of light — and a vision of What Could Have Been.

name: Killing Is a Cycle
overview: A brewing storm fuels a series of startling transformations. Elsewhere, the spark of rebellion still burns.

name: The Dirt Under Your Nails
overview: Magic. Science. Power. Revenge. Destinies clash in an epic final chapter, igniting an all-out war.

name: Stars In Fast Cars
overview:
The Top Gear special feature for Sports Relief, first broadcast on 10 July 2004

Top Gear's Jeremy Clarkson went bumper to bumper with Jodie Kidd, Alan Davies, Patrick Kielty, Darren Jordan and Jimmy Carr in a series of tyre-screechingly fast paced challenges designed to test their pedal power to the max.

Among the highlights were a classic car race around the circuit, car-skittles and a double-decker banger challenge.

Richard Hammond and James May presided over the action and offered their usual critique on contestants' skill levels.

name: Series 4 Best of
overview: Highlights from Season 4

name: Series 5 Best of (1)
overview: Highlights from Season 5

name: Series 5 Best Of (4)
overview: In this special compilation Clarkson prepares to see if he can get a diesel Jaguar S-Type around the Nürburgring in under ten minutes, Richard puts the Pagani Zonda S Roadster through its paces in the South of France and the presenters find themselves each buying a Porsche for £1500, and put them through a series of tests to see who got the best deal.

name: Best of Series 5 & 6
overview: In this compilation of the best of series 5 and 6, Richard Hammond heads to the south of France to put the Pagani Zonda S Roadster to the test, James May test drives his favourite Italian 70's supercar, the Maserati Bora, Roger Daltry is the star in our reasonably priced car and Jeremy Clarkson finds out how good the Mitsubishi Lancer Evolution VIII MR FQ400 is, by having it take on the Lamborghini Murciélago driven by British touring cap champion, James Kaye.

name: Best of Series 6 & 7
overview: All the best bits from series 6 and 7. The presenters race each other to Oslo, with Jeremy driving the entire distance overland whilst Richard and James take public transport. Clarkson reviews the Ford Focus ST and Hammond discovers how many sideways rolls can be made with a Ford Sierra estate. Stars in a reasonably priced car Davina McCall, Christopher Eccleston.

name: Best of: The Special Guests
overview:
Carr: Jimmy Carr takes us on a short tour of the Top Gear production offices, green room, make-up, etc.

Challenge: Clarkson, in an Audi RS4, vs. speed mountain-climbers, at a gorge in France, from Series 7, Episode 2

Carr: Jimmy tours The Stig's room, then goes to the track, where Walliams is doing his laps, and interview The Stig

Challenge: Hammond joins Sabine Schmitz at the Nürburgring, where she attempts a lap of less than ten minutes, in a Transit van, from Series 6, Episode 7

Carr: Jimmy tours the car park, commenting on the cars held therein. He then interviews May, who relates a tale of being "the other guy".

Challenge: Clarkson drives the Mercedes SLK55 AMG and Porsche Boxster S around a deserted village, whilst being shot at be an elite 8-man sniper squadron, from Series 6, Episode 5

Reviews: Hamster and Slow play "Road Test Roulette", reviewing the cars of drunk people, from Series 6, Episode 9

Carr: The crowd is interviewed, and The Stig bothered.

Review: Clarkson tests the Aston Martin Vanquish S in a race against Steve Coogan, driving his Ferrari F575, from Series 5, Episode 4

name: Best of Series 7 The Challenges!
overview: This special compilation of the best ever challenges sees Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May go 70s retro in 10,000 pound supercars. Also, Jeremy takes on a tank in a Range Rover Sport, Richard tries to out-sprint a champion greyhound in a Mazda MX5 and James 'Captain Slow' May goes all extreme and takes on a BMX biker around Lisbon in a Renault Clio. Plus we find out what is the best convertible for wig wearers.

name: Series 8 Best of (1)
overview:
The presenters are tasked turning a car into an amphibious vehicle and capturing the public's imagination – Hammond buys a 1983 Volkswagen T3 campervan and turns it into a houseboat, May converts a 1962 Triumph Herald into a sailing craft with a mast and some sails, and Clarkson uses a 1989 Toyota Hilux pick-up truck and plants a huge outboard motor on its back. The trio soon see how their creations fare on the road, before taking them onto the waters of Rudyard Lake near Leek, Staffordshire and crossing two miles of it, where one of the presenters has a sinking feeling about their amphibious car. Meanwhile, Hammond is in Iceland to race a man with powered canoe against a specially prepped 4x4 Tomcat.
Stars in a reasonably priced car: Ewan McGregor, Gordan Ramsay. Cars reviewed: Koenigsegg CCX

name: Best of Series 5 - 8
overview: In this special compilation Clarkson is in a Volvo XC90 in a "Biathlon", competing against May in an Audi Q7, as they endure a cross-country course complete with 2 shooting rounds, before trying out the new Range Rover Sport to see if he can avoid being locked on by the main cannon of a Challenger 2 tank. A world champion all-terrain skateboarder is pitted against a Group N Mitsubishi rally car, May is in Dubai and driving the Bentley Continental Flying Spur, Clarkson races against the climber Leo Houlding to the top of the Verdon Gorge in France in an Audi RS4, before trying to drive up a mountain in a Land Rover Discovery.

name: Best of Series 3 - 8
overview: In this special compilation Clarkson sees how tough "that" Toyota Hilux pick-up truck truly is by putting it through a series of tests, including driving it through Bristol and nearly losing it to the sea, before returning to the test track to see what else it can withstand. Hammond finds out if he can get the same thrill and excitement he got from participating in the Pamplona Bull Run in Spain, in the Lamborghini Murciélago Roadster. Clarkson also tries to see if he can beat a human skater in the "Speed Skating" event, in a Jaguar XKR. The trio take a look at what it's like to drive vans, as they take a Renault Master, a Ford Transit and a Volkswagen T30 TDI 174 Sportline, and become roadies for The Who. Clarkson claims he's found a good way to give up smoking with the new £415,000 Koenigsegg CCX. Hammond competes against May in a game of "Car Ice Hockey" with several Suzuki Swifts. Finally May sees what else can be done to prove that a Toyota Hilux is tough by placing the same Hilux on top of a tower block due for demolition.

name: Series 9 Best of
overview: Summary of the best clips from Season 9

name: Top Gear of the Pops
overview:
Top Gear of the Pops is a one-off musical special of the motoring show featuring Travis, Supergrass, McFly (given the task of writing a song in ten minutes) and finally Justin Hawkins - supported by Clarkson on drums, Hammond on bass and May on the keys.

It was shown as part of the Comic Relief 16 March 2007 appeal, mixing elements of Top Gear with Top of the Pops, the British music chart show cancelled in 2006, and the news segment features the hosts arguing about the state of the modern music scene.  Not to be missed.

name: Series 10 Best of (4)
overview: A selection of highlights from the 2007 series, including an experiment conducted to determine the fastest way of travelling across London during peak time. James May decides whether the Rolls Royce Phantom Drophead Coupe deserves the title of the world's most luxurious car, while Jeremy Clarkson puts the speedy Ascari A10 through its paces. With Simon Cowell and Lawrence Dallaglio featuring in Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Top Ground Gear Force
overview:
The Top Gear team of James May, Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond swap the steering wheel for the shovel as they embark on a Ground Force style makeover challenge as part of Sport Relief.

The Top Gear team of Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond decide, for Sport Relief to resurrect the show Ground Force, and provide a garden makeover to the garden of one of Britain's 'sporting legends', namely Sir Stephen Redgrave. Helping them are a team of Poles.

As the team's only 'country bumpkin' Hammond is the team leader. However, his garden plan fails to impress Clarkson and May, who want to include a water feature and a shed, respectively. They are sent to a Wyevale Garden Centre to pick up supplies, returning with their desired equipment.

Hammond uses a small digger to dig a trench, to be used to create a 'river of gravel.' Clarkson earlier revealed his unique method for pulling up weeds and cutting branches off trees using a shotgun. Hammond's digger topples over in the trench, so he returns with a much larger digger to pull it out, resulting in leaving numerous Caterpillar tracks marks all over the garden. Meanwhile, James and the Poles have completed the shed.

Hammond's tree seat is completed. He brings in a 14 tone crane to hook the seat so he could slip it over the tree. Despite the machine getting stuck, the crane is long enough to hook onto the seat. Because he had not got the hang of the crane, he accidentally swished it sideways, destroying both the shed and the seat.

Clarkson decides to remove a rockery by using a homemade bomb. During the explosion one wall of the shed James built is smashed. After this happens May steps out of the shed with a plank of wood, and asks what time the programme will air. After being informed it was going to air after the watershed, he exclaims 'you're a fu-'. At this point, the episode cuts to the next scene.

Jeremy orders some cement to pave over the garden, but he orders too little, and only a small

name: Series 11 Best of
overview: Highlights from Season 11

name: Series 12 Best of (1)
overview:
Richard Hammond and James May travel to the Riviera to race a classic Ferrari Daytona against a state of the art powerboat. 

Meanwhile, Jeremy Clarkson conducts a 'serious' road test of the new Ford Fiesta - with a little help from the Royal Marines.

name: Series 12 Best of (2)
overview: A special featuring a selection of highlights from Top Gear in 2008. Jeremy, James and Richard fall in love with three old Alfa Romeos when they take them on a road trip across England. Also in the show, Richard discovers the possible future of personal transport in Japan whilst James May conducts a power test of the new Zonda F convertible hypercar. The star in the reasonably priced car is Will Young.

name: Series 12 Best of (3)
overview: Motoring news and views from the usual team. Jeremy, James and Richard travel to Belgium to do battle with the German presenters of D Motor - Top Gear's German equivalent. Also in the show, Richard travels to the Alps where he races his Audi RS6 against two extreme speed skiers, and Jeremy travels to the countryside to go foxhunting in a Daihatsu Terios. The star in a reasonably priced car is the Mayor of London, Boris Johnson.

name: Series 12 Best of (4)
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond travel to Japan for an epic race between the Nissan GTR and the Bullet Train. Also in the show, Jeremy takes the awesome Mercedes CLK Black for a test drive in Wales and James heads to California where he discovers that Honda have saved the world. The star in the reasonably priced car is Jay Kay.

name: Series 13 and 14 Best of (1)
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May star in a compilation of their finest moments from 2009 featuring amazing challenges, spectacular stunts and a small amount of bickering. Plus The Stig attacks the track in a new sports car and there is another chance to see a classic celebrity guest in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Middle East Special
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on an appropriately seasonal road trip as they attempt to follow the path of the Three Wise Men across the Middle East.

name: Best of Season 16 (1)
overview: Highlights of season 16, including a test drive in Albania with a mafia theme, Clarkson test drives the Skoda Yeti and compares it in a number of unusual challenges and John Bishop is the Star in a Reasonably Car

name: Best of Season 16 (2)
overview: Highlights of season 16, including Top Gears attempt at dealing with snow on the roads by converting a combine harvester, Clarkson test drives the Zonda R and Jonathan Ross is the the Star in a Reasonably Car

name: India Special
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set off on a trade mission across India, equipped with three old British cars and a range of uniquely British products.

name: Top Fails (1)
overview: Catch Richard Hammond as he hosts an all-new BBC America original two-part special – a celebration of  ‘How hard can it be?’ challenges of the many seasons of the ‘Top Gear’ crew. The specials include some of the most memorable, and rubbish challenges, as well as many lesser-known, but no-less destructive, adventures. The result is a comical look back at all that ‘Top Gear’ has tried to accomplish with Richard sharing the laughs of these spectacular fails

name: Top Fails (2)
overview: Catch Richard Hammond as he hosts an all-new BBC America original two-part special – a celebration of  ‘How hard can it be?’ challenges of the many seasons of the ‘Top Gear’ crew. The specials include some of the most memorable, and rubbish challenges, as well as many lesser-known, but no-less destructive, adventures. The result is a comical look back at all that ‘Top Gear’ has tried to accomplish with Richard sharing the laughs of these spectacular fails.

name: 50 Years of Bond Cars: A Top Gear Special
overview: Richard celebrates 50 years of James Bond films with a look back at the film's different vehicles, including the Aston Martin DB5 and the Lotus Esprit. He also chats to Daniel Craig and Roger Moore and goes behind the scenes on the latest Bond adventure Skyfall.

name: Top Gear: Uncovered
overview: Richard Hammond's Top Gear - Uncovered, explores behind the scenes of the show, from his unique perspective.

name: Best of Season 19 (1)
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May star in a compilation of their finest moments from Series 19 featuring amazing challenges, spectacular stunts and a small amount of bickering. Plus another chance to see a classic celebrity guest in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Best of Season 19 (2)
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May star in a compilation of their finest moments from Series 19, featuring amazing challenges, spectacular stunts and a small amount of bickering. Plus another chance to see a classic celebrity guest in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Top Gear Festival: Sydney
overview: The Top Gear Festival special kicks off with Clarkson and May smoking V8 race utes to settle the Holden vs Ford debate, then Mark Webber turns up.

name: Best of Series 20 and Series 21 (4)
overview: Another choice compilation of horsepower, tyre smoke and calamity as Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May engage in more high-octane horseplay.

name: Ambitious But Rubbish (1)
overview: Host Richard Hammond looks back through the Top Gear archive at some memorable moments of ineptitude that illustrate the Top Gear phrase “Ambitious but Rubbish.” The premiere episode includes the creation of the world’s first convertible minivan, James May attempting to drive up an Icelandic Volcano with an unpronounceable name, and the boys have a go at building some budget police cars.

name: Ambitious But Rubbish (2)
overview: In the second episode, host Richard Hammond guides viewers through another batch of stunts and challenges from past episodes of Top Gear. Highlights include an attempt to cross the English Channel in homemade amphibious cars and building off-road mobility scooters.

name: Ambitious But Rubbish (3)
overview: Host Richard Hammond continues to re-live moments from the world’s biggest car show in Top Gear: Ambitious But Rubbish. Tonight’s episode includes an attempt to find the best bus for London’s streets, building a caravan airship, and creating a dual purpose combine harvester-based snow plough.

name: Ambitious But Rubbish (4)
overview: Host Richard Hammond shares more disastrous outcomes from the history of Top Gear, including building the world’s first hovercraft van, attempting to solve the thorny issue of CO2 emissions from cars, as well as having a go at launching a Reliant Robin into space.

name: Ambitious But Rubbish (5)
overview: This week, viewers get to re-live the Top Gear team’s attempts to constructing a V8-powered rocking chair, building Top Gear’s own electric car, and making the world’s first caravan-based train.

name: Ambitious But Rubbish (6)
overview: Richard Hammond continues to present unforgettable moments from the Top Gear archive. Tonight’s highlights include creating stretch limos based on European cars, giving a Mercedes S Class a country cottage style make-over, and Jeremy Clarkson making a public information film.

name: Ambitious But Rubbish (7)
overview: Tonight’s episode looks back at some of Top Gear’s most inept moments which include creating a V8-powered food blender and building a Caterham car in a race against the Stig.

name: Ambitious But Rubbish (8)
overview: In the season finale, host Richard Hammond guides viewers through another batch of the most disastrous outcomes from past seasons of Top Gear. Tonight’s episode includes building an old, people-friendly car, creating motorhomes more suited to British roads, and making a public information film for cycle safety.

name: Patagonia Special (1)
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May take three sports cars on a gruelling 1,600-mile journey through the spectacular landscapes of Patagonia.

name: Patagonia Special (2)
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May continue in their quest to take three V8-engined sports cars on an epic journey through Patagonia.

name: An Evening With Top Gear
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May exclusively preview the forthcoming new series of Top Gear in front of a studio audience and online fans from around the world at a special event in London.

name: Best of Series 22 (1)
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James feature in a compilation of their finest moments from series 22

name: From A-Z (1)
overview: John Bishop narrates the first programme of this two-part alphabetical celebration of the best of the Clarkson, Hammond and May years.

name: From A-Z (2)
overview: John Bishop narrates the second programme of this two-part alphabetical celebration of the best of the Clarkson, Hammond and May years.

name: Top Gear: The Races (1)
overview:
From very early on in Top Gear's life the phrase 'We decided to have a race' has appeared often. Some of the races have been brief, and some have been simply epic. Matt LeBlanc, one of the fastest ever celebrities round the Top Gear test track, presents a selection of the greatest and most entertaining races from the past 13 years.
Episode 1 shows an Aston Martin DB9 against the Eurostar to Monte Carlo; a Bugatti Veyron vs a Eurofighter; and a rental car against an America's Cup yacht in New Zealand.

name: Top Gear: The Races (2)
overview:
From very early on in Top Gear's life the phrase 'We decided to have a race' has appeared often. Some of the races have been brief, and some have been simply epic. Matt LeBlanc, one of the fastest ever celebrities round the Top Gear test track, presents a selection of the greatest and most entertaining races from the past 13 years.

Matt LeBlanc guides us through another batch of races, including an Alfa Romeo 4C against an amphibious ATV Quad in the Italian Lakes, a four-way race across London and a Porsche 911 Turbo vs the unstoppable force of gravity.

name: Top Gear: The Races (3)
overview:
From very early on in Top Gear's life the phrase 'We decided to have a race' has appeared often. Some of the races have been brief, and some have been simply epic. Matt LeBlanc, one of the fastest ever celebrities round the Top Gear test track, presents a selection of the greatest and most entertaining races from the past 13 years.

Another look through the archive with Matt LeBlanc, featuring a WRC Mini rally car against an Olympic gold medalist on a skeleton bob, a Bugatti Veyron against James May in a Cessna, and a VW Toureg vs some snowmobilers.

name: Top Gear: The Races (4)
overview:
From very early on in Top Gear's life the phrase 'We decided to have a race' has appeared often. Some of the races have been brief, and some have been simply epic. Matt LeBlanc, one of the fastest ever celebrities round the Top Gear test track, presents a selection of the greatest and most entertaining races from the past 13 years.

Another batch of the most entertaining races in Top Gear's history including a classic Ferrari Daytona against a million pound powerboat to St Tropez, a jet-powered flying man against Richard Hammond in a rally car, an Audi RS4 vs a speed climber and Jeremy vs the might of the British Army.

name: Top Gear: The Races (5)
overview:
From very early on in Top Gear's life the phrase 'We decided to have a race' has appeared often. Some of the races have been brief, and some have been simply epic. Matt LeBlanc, one of the fastest ever celebrities round the Top Gear test track, presents a selection of the greatest and most entertaining races from the past 13 years.

Matt LeBlanc looks back at more amazing races from the archive, including James May in a Mistubishi Evo rally car vs Richard Hammond in a bobsleigh, a steam train vs a classic Jaguar and a Vincent motorcycle, and James May races a pair of free-runners across a Liverpool in a car v parkour face-off.

name: Top Gear: The Races (6)
overview:
From very early on in Top Gear's life the phrase 'We decided to have a race' has appeared often. Some of the races have been brief, and some have been simply epic. Matt LeBlanc, one of the fastest ever celebrities round the Top Gear test track, presents a selection of the greatest and most entertaining races from the past 13 years.

In this batch of the best races from the Top Gear archive, can a Ferrari 612 beat public transport in a marathon to the French Alps? Plus, Jeremy races the sun itself in a supercharged Jaguar, and a driverless military vehicle from the U.S. faces the latest Range Rover.

name: Top Gear: The Races (7)
overview:
From very early on in Top Gear's life the phrase 'We decided to have a race' has appeared often. Some of the races have been brief, and some have been simply epic. Matt LeBlanc, one of the fastest ever celebrities round the Top Gear test track, presents a selection of the greatest and most entertaining races from the past 13 years.

Highlights from the final episode include Jeremy Clarkson in a Nissan GTR vs a Japanese Bullet Train, Richard Hammond's experiments in the new sport of 'Motorhome Racing', and a Porsche Cayenne Turbo takes on a wingsuit flyer.

name: Best Of (1)
overview:
Featuring the greatest Top Gear adventures from the last three series. 
This week the boys head to Kazakhstan to test three very high-mileage motors, and Matt makes a special bond with an Aston Martin before getting dusty in the desert in the Ariel Nomad.

name: Best Of (2)
overview:
Featuring the greatest Top Gear adventures from the last three series.
This week Chris and Matt test two all-season supercars, Chris gets sideways in an Alfa Romeo, Rory goes retro-arcade gaming in a Renault Twingo, and Matt gets to grips with a go-anywhere SUV from Russia.

name: Best Of (3)
overview: Featuring the greatest Top Gear adventures from the last three series. Chris tests two new sports cars from Japan, Matt challenges Chris to a very expensive race, Chris shows Matt the charms of the Citroen 2CV, and Rory has a special plan for the world's ugliest car.

name: Best Of (4)
overview: Featuring the greatest Top Gear adventures from the last three series. This week Chris takes on Sabine in the world's toughest off-road race, and Matt puts the Dodge Demon through its paces at Nasa and then speeds up farming with an all-new tractor.

name: Best Of (5)
overview: Featuring the greatest Top Gear adventures from the last three series. Matt takes Chris on a hunt for Bigfoot, Rory delves into Japan's underground car scene, Chris tears up the tarmac in the Ferrari FXX K and Matt goes on a very fast sightseeing trip around London.

name: Nepal Special
overview: Paddy, Freddie and Chris set out on a high-altitude voyage from Kathmandu to the Forbidden City of Lo Manthang, through the highest mountains, deepest gorges and harshest terrain on the planet.

name: Best of Series 27 and 28 (1)
overview: In their first adventure together, Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the searing heat of Ethiopia in their first cars. Chris races the £2.5m McLaren Speedtail against the RAF’s newest fighter jet.

name: Best of Series 27 and 28 (2)
overview: More of the best bits from the last two series. Freddie tries to bungee-jump a car off a dam, Paddy sets out to prove an old hearse is the ultimate family car, and Chris remembers the iconic Lotus 79 race car.

name: Best of Series 27 and 28 (3)
overview:
More of the best bits from the last two series.

The team enjoy a crashy summer road trip in £500 convertibles, before Chris and Freddie head to Iceland to take part in the brutal motorsport of Formula Off Road. The Dallara Stradale takes to the Top Gear track.

name: Best of Series 27 and 28 (4)
overview: More of the best bits from the last two series. Freddie, Chris and Paddy tackle the highlands of Peru in some old American road-trip cars. Chris tests the jaw-dropping, all-electric ID-R race car.

name: Best of Series 27 and 28 (5)
overview: More of the best bits from the last two series. Freddie, Chris and Paddy build their own electric sports cars for the inaugural 24 Minutes of Le Mansfield race. Chris tests the all-electric Porsche Taycan, and there is a tribute to the legendary Colin McRae.

name: Best of Series 27 and 28 (6)
overview: More of the best bits from the last two series. Paddy McGuinness builds his own luxurious off-roader in the shape of the Dirty Rascal, Chris samples the Rolls-Royce Cullinan, and there is a Yorkshire road trip in the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche.

name: Behind the Scenes
overview: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness look back at spending 24 hours in cars, off-roading in Cyprus, and taming the Wall of Death during the 2020 series of Top Gear.

name: A Tribute to Sabine Schmitz
overview: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris, Paddy McGuinness and former hosts, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond, James May, Matt LeBlanc and Rory Reid, join well-known figures from the world of motoring to share their fondest memories of their colleague Sabine, Queen of the Nurburgring.

name: Best of Series 29 and 30 (1)
overview:
Another chance to see some highlights of the recent series featuring Chris Harris, Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness messing around in cars.

Freddie attempts to drive 200mph in a Jaguar XJ220, the team try out some of James Bond's greatest cars, and the Lamborghini Sian gets a test drive.

name: Best of Series 29 and 30 (2)
overview: Chris, Freddie and Paddy attempt to drive insurance write-offs around a terrifying Wall of Death, Chris reviews the Ferrari SF90, and the boys race around Alton Towers in electric cars.

name: Best of Series 29 and 30 (3)
overview: In tonight’s episode, another outing for Paddy’s monster Mr Nippy ice cream van, a tribute to Sir Stirling Moss, the Top Gear Mid-Life Crisis Triathlon and two very different takes on Italian supercars.

name: Best of Series 29 and 30 (4)
overview: The presenters are challenged to spend 24 hours in company cars in Paddy’s home town of Bolton, and Freddie races an Extreme E electric off-roader against a man in a jet pack.

name: Driving Home for Christmas
overview: In this special Christmas episode, Freddie, Chris and Paddy play 'car secret Santa' for a festive road trip across Britain, transporting a giant Christmas tree as they head to Top Gear's very own winter wonderland.

name: Best of Series 31 and 32 (1)
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy set out to revolutionise the world of caravanning with the help of some new electric cars and some very small caravans. Chris lives the high life in Maserati’s new MC20 supercar, and Paddy pays tribute to the legendary Eddie Kidd.

name: Best of Series 31 and 32 (2)
overview: Paddy reviews the very rare, very expensive Aston Martin Victor. The team sample some classic TV cop cars before going head-to-head in an all-action police chase, and Paddy and Chris set out on an expedition across Iceland with the help of some temperamental old British cars.

name: Best of Series 31 and 32 (3)
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy head to the British Grand Prix for a showdown with a trio of F1 drivers. Freddie transforms a Sinclair C5 into a bobsleigh, and Chris tells the story of the DMC DeLorean.

name: Best of Series 31 and 32 (4)
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy set out to find interesting old cars for new drivers, then put them to the test on a road trip through Devon and Cornwall.

name: Best of Series 3
overview: Highlights compilation of Series 3, including the watery end of Top Gear's old Jaguar, a special from the Isle of Man, and the arrival of the new Stig.

name: East Coast Road Trip
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May compare the Mercedes SLS, the Porsche 911 GT3 RS and the Ferrari 458 Italia in an epic road trip up America's east coast.

name: Best of British
overview: The team present a special selection of items from the vaults celebrating the best of British, including putting a range of British cars through their paces.

name: Polar Challenge
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond face an arduous challenge as they attempt to race from northern Canada to the North Pole, a 450-mile journey.

name: Top Gear: Apocalypse
overview: Just what will motoring be like in a post apocalyptic world that's been blown to bits by nuclear bombs or a massive comet? It's a burning question that's on literally nobody's mind, and we give you all the answers in this brand new, incredibly cheerful Top Gear DVD. Embarking on a terrifying journey into the future, we show you how to drive to work in the perpetual darkness of a Nuclear Winter, how to make motor sport exciting when there's only two racing drivers left alive, and, in a world where all cars are bristling with weapons, how to survive the savage, explosive fury of a Doomsday M.O.T test. And amongst all the nuclear carnage, we also tackle the ultimate petrolhead's dilemma - what cars would you take for a final drive if there was only one barrel of petrol left on the planet? The explosions are huge, the cars are superb, so relax and enjoy. PS. This frightening vision of a post nuclear world is backed up by top scientists, in case you think it's us just cocking about.

name: Best of: The Supercars
overview: A look back to some of the best moments from the last seven series. Highlights include the best moments with supercars driven on Top Gear, including the Bugatti Veyron vs. Cessna Aeroplane race, the Aston Martin DB9 vs. Train race, as well as a compilation of moments from the Stig's Power Laps and Cool Wall segments.

name: Top Gear: The Worst Car in the History of the World
overview:
Jeremy Clarkson and James May set out to find the worst car in the history of the world — but with one key rule: it has to be an expensive car from a manufacturer that really should have known better. Cheap Eastern European rust buckets or obvious low-budget disasters don't count.
They review and roast various contenders (including the FSO Polonez, some odd Jeep copies, Ferrari Mondial, Ford GT for weird reasons, and others), but ultimately crown the Lexus SC 430 (2001–2010 model, known in some markets as Toyota Soarer) as the worst car in the history of the world. The special ends with a symbolic "prize" for the loser: handing the car over to Richard Hammond to destroy/play with as scrap.

name: Top Gear: The Perfect Road Trip
overview: Seven days, more than 1,000 miles, 20 cars, two boats, a motorbike and one dream...The Perfect Road Trip. Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond choose their transport for each leg of a pilgrimage from Venice, Italy to Pau in France - home to a legendary street circuit and the origins of Grand Prix racing. Their dream drives include the Ferrari F12 Berlinetta, Mercedes SLS Black and Aston Martin Vanquish. Then it's time to turn up the heat even more with a Top Gear race... and the worst forfeit in television history.

name: Top Gear: The Perfect Road Trip 2
overview: In 2013 Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond embarked on what they hoped would be the perfect road trip. It started well and ended, quite frankly, very badly. Unbowed, the Top Gear pair are back for another crack and this time they're hoping to avoid inconveniences like apprehension by the French police. Welcome, then, to The Perfect Road Trip... Italy. Once again, Clarkson and Hammond are seeking joy and perfection wherever it may be with a range of fast, beautiful and exciting cars unleashed on glorious roads amongst gorgeous scenery and drenched in Mediterranean sunshine. As part of their arduous research into perfection, the duo will also undertake some ridiculous challenges and hilarious stunts culminating in a strangely literal car race on the island of Capri. Top Gear - The Perfect Road Trip Italy. This time it really is perfect. Except for the bits that aren't.

name: Top Gear: At the Movies
overview: Cars are the bedrock of the most memorable moments in cinema history, so be prepared for a thrilling selection of races, stunts and awesome feats as Richard Hammond and James May celebrate cars on the big screen through a blend of action, amazing driving and exotic metal.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe review the Citroen Berlingo, Ford GT40 and Mazda 6, and talk to Harry Enfield.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe are joined by special guest Jay Kay. Features Ford Focus RS, Insider Dealing and RS 1800.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson road tests the Aston Martin DB7. Jason Dawe checks out two second-hand cars. Richard Hammond looks at the Citroen DS.

name: Episode 4
overview: Featuring the Aston Martin Vanquish, Ferrari 575, Nissan Skyline and cars from the new Bond film.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe review the S Class Mercedes, Audi A8, Jaguar XJ and Bentley Arnage T.

name: Episode 6
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and Jason Dawe welcome special guest Tara Palmer-Tomkinson. The theme is stylish cars and the Top Gear Cool Wall is launched.

name: Episode 7
overview: Richard Burns sizes up the diesel powered Peugeot RC sports car and the Lotus Elise is tested.

name: Episode 8
overview: The theme is driver's cars. Featuring Sir Michael Gambon, and including Lada into Lotus, white van man and Maserati.

name: Episode 9
overview: Including a look at the Subaru Forester, Cool Wall, Insider Dealing and a review of the Golf R32.

name: Episode 10
overview: The team introduce the Car of the Year awards, and look at the latest German cars and the Lotus Espirit.

name: Episode 1
overview: Richard continues on his quest to destroy as many caravans as possible, and James May makes his debut. Tough-man Vinnie Jones is the Star In A Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy test drives the new Rolls-Royce, and various politicians race in a bid to win the dubious title of Fastest Political Party. Jamie Oliver is in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Richard drives the Lexus SC430 and Jeremy tests the VW Touareg – is it really worth the high price tag? Plus David Soul in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jaguars are the theme, with a look at everything from the futuristic R Coupe concept car to the iconic MKII. With Boris Johnson in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy test drives the new Porsche 911 C4s, and there's a closer look at the Triumph TR6 and Renault Clio V6. Anne Robinson is the Star In A Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 6
overview: James tries to set a new record for towing a caravan, and the Peugeot 206GTi and the Vauxhall VX 220 turbo are tested. Richard Whiteley is in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 7
overview: A Renault Megane has a test crash, with a real person inside, and the Hummer H2 and Talon Riot Control Vehicle are put through their paces. With Neil Morrissey.

name: Episode 8
overview: James and Richard enjoy camping in the wettest part of the UK, using a variety of Cabriolets, while Jeremy tests the Alpha Romeo 147 GTA. Jodie Kidd is the guest.

name: Episode 9
overview: The show looks to the future, with a focus on new concept vehicles such as the revolutionary Hy-wire. Patrick Stewart is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 10
overview: The trio test the VW Phaeton, an enormous Cadillac Sixteen prototype, and a super-fast TVR T350c. Alan Davies guest stars.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy reviews the Ford GT and VW Lupo, Richard tries the Porsche GT3, and James tests out the new-look BMW 5 series. With Martin Kemp in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 2
overview: The team test the BMW Z4, Porsche Boxster and Honda S2000 to decide which is the best roadster, and the new Stig tests the BMW M3 CSL. Stephen Fry drives the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Safety is the focus, with James testing the Subaru Legacy estate and Richard attempting to escape from a submerged car. Rob Brydon drives the Reasonably Priced Car – a Suzuki Liana.

name: Episode 4
overview: The subject this week is the Lamborghini, with the Countach, Gallardo and Miura all taken for a spin. With Rich Hall as our Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy drives a Toyota Hilux to test its claims of indestructability, and the Mazda RX8 is also in the spotlight. With Simon Cowell in our Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 6
overview: Richard tests the Coupé Cabriolets from Renault and Peugeot, Jeremy drives the Citroen C2 and Vauxhall Monaro, and James has a spin in an Aston Martin Vantage. With Sanjeev Bhaskar.

name: Episode 7
overview: The Porsche Cayenne is taken off-road, Richard heads to South Africa to test the Mercedes McClaren SLR, and the search is on for best British car. With Rory Bremner.

name: Episode 8
overview: Cars from the past are compared to the cars of today, with a drag-race featuring the original Golf GTI and the new Toyota MR2. With Johnny Vegas in our Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 9
overview: Richard tests a Smart car, Jeremy is unimpressed with the Chrysler Crossfire, and James gets to drive a Jaguar XJ6. With guest star Carol Vorderman.

name: Episode 1
overview: The trio race from London to Monte Carlo, with Richard and James using high-speed trains and Jeremy on the road in an Aston Martin DB9. With special guest Fay Ripley.

name: Episode 2
overview: The team check out the Cadillac Escalade and an Alfa Romeo 166, and a nun drives a monster truck. Paul McKenna hypnotises Richard, and is the Star In A Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: The trio are challenged to find a functioning car for £100 or less, and the Porsche GT3 RS and Ferrari 360 CS compete on the race track. Jordan is in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy is challenged to drive 800 miles on one tank of petrol in an Audio V8 Quattro, while James wonders if homing pigeons are better than satnavs. With guest Ronnie O' Sullivan.

name: Episode 5
overview: The BMW 645i, Porsche 911 Carrera 2 and Jaguar XKR are put to the test on the sand dunes of Wales. Denise Van Outen and Johnny Vaughan are in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 6
overview: Richard wonders if the cars of the future could be run on methane, and Jeremy tests the Nissan Cube. Terry Wogan is this week's Star In A Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 7
overview: The new tall hatchbacks, the Ford Focus C-Max and Renault Scenic, are put to the test as minicabs in south London. Lionel Ritchie is this week’s Star In A Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 8
overview: The trio look at the new Ford GT40, Maserati Quattroporte and Toyota Prius, and have a diesel versus petrol hot hatch race. Martin Clunes is this week’s guest star.

name: Episode 9
overview: The focus is on convertibles, featuring the Mini Cabrio, Vauxhall Tigra, Mercedes SLK and the Audi S4. With guest star Sir Ranulph Fiennes.

name: Episode 10
overview: In the final episode of the series, Richard races a Peugeot 407, James is bargain hunting in a Volvo V50, and Jeremy tests out the new BMW X3. With guest star Patrick Kielty.

name: Episode 1
overview: The trio test cars from the colonies - an Australian Vauxhall Monaro VXR, a Chrysler 300c from the USA and the UK's Jaguar S Type R. With Bill Bailey in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 2
overview: Supercars past and present is the theme, with a Pagani Zonda, Porsche Carrera GT and Ferrari Enzo racing the Jaguar XJ220, McLaren F1 and Ferrari F40. With Geri Halliwell.

name: Episode 3
overview: The Landrover Discovery and Dodge Viper SRT-10 are given a rough time by Jeremy, and Richard tries an amphibious concept vehicle by Rinspeed. With guest star Joanna Lumley.

name: Episode 4
overview: Two cars face each other at 200mph. Which will come out on top, the Aston Martin Vanquish S or the Ferrari 575? There's fun with a game of car conkers, and Jimmy Carr guests.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy pushes the diesel engine in the new Jaguar S Type to its limits, and the trio laugh at a Morgan Aero 8GTN. With guest Christian Slater in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 6
overview: The trio are handed £1500 each to buy a Porsche - but who will find the best one? Plus a look at the VW Golf GTI, and Cliff Richard in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 7
overview: The Lamborghini Murcielago is up against the Mitsubishi EVO VIII, and Jeremy tests a Toyota Prius. Roger Daltrey is the Star In A Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 8
overview: Richard and James try to beat Jeremy in a contest of car versus plane, with Jeremy driving the Ferrari 612 Scaglietti. With guest Eddie Izzard in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 9
overview: Jeremy enjoys his experience with an Ariel Atom, and Richard declares the Mercedes G Wagen the best car ever. With guest stars Trinny and Susannah.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy squares up against a Challenger 2 tank in the new Range Rover Sport.

name: Episode 2
overview: The team find out if you can buy a stylish two-door coupe for less than 1,500 pounds.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy opens a local swimming pool, and James goes back to the 70s with the Maserati Bora.

name: Episode 4
overview: The guys hand over duties to their mothers to review a range of new small cars.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy comes under fire from army snipers to deduce which is better - the Porsche Boxster S or Mercedes SLK.

name: Episode 6
overview: A race from London to Oslo with Jeremy in the Mercedes McLaren SLR and Richard and James on a boat.

name: Episode 7
overview: Good news for white van men as the Ford Transit has a landmark birthday.

name: Episode 8
overview: A trio of convertibles head for Iceland - the Audi TT, Nissan 350Z and Chrysler Crossfire; a new car from Ferrari - the F430; and this week's 'Star in a Reasonably Priced Car' is Tim Rice.

name: Episode 9
overview: Jeremy gives his verdict on BMW's new M5; Richard and James try their hand at Russian roulette road testing; and which hatchback is the hottest - the Renault Megane, Vauxhall Astra VXR or Golf GTi?

name: Episode 10
overview: Including a look at the latest range of off-road toys and the fastest four-door saloon in the world - the Bentley Flying Spur. Also, how a BMW 5 series diesel measures up against the 5 series petrol, and a jeep attempts to drive across a lake. Davina McCall is 'Star in a Reasonably Priced Car'.

name: Episode 11
overview: The team find out if driving a Lamborghini Murcielago roadster gives the same kick as 12 angry bulls; the world's greatest power sliders grace the track; and a look at the Ford F150 SVT Lightning pick-up. Timothy Spall is this week's 'Star in a Reasonably Priced Car'.

name: Episode 1
overview: The team head for the Isle of Man to decide which is best - the V8 Vantage Aston Martin, BMW M6 or Porsche 911 Carrera S. The Ascari KZ1supercar takes to the track and the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car is Trevor Eve.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy finds out whether a rock climber is faster than the new Audi RS4.

name: Episode 3
overview: The chaps take the Ford GT, Ferrari F430 and Pagani Zonda on a road trip.

name: Episode 4
overview: The team try to buy a decent mid-engined Italian supercar for under 10,000 pounds.

name: Episode 5
overview: A challenge between Jeremy in a Bugatti Veyron against the boys in a light aircraft flown by Captain Slow. Alan Titchmarsh is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 6
overview: Is Jeremy faster driving in real life or on a computer game, and what will break first at sub zero temperatures - a car or Richard Hammond? David Walliams is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Winter Olympics Special
overview: The team hit Norway for a Winter Olympics special in which they attempt Olympic events, but with cars. The team fire a rocket-powered Mini off a ski jump.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy drives a powerful Swedish supercar, Richard races a jet-powered canoe and the boys create the world's first convertible people carrier by sawing the top off a Renault Espace.

name: Episode 2
overview: The Yorkshire Dales are the backdrop for a contest between the Jaguar XK, the BMW650i and the Mercedes SL350.

name: Episode 3
overview: The team attempt to build a truly amphibious car that works as well on water as it is does on land.

name: Episode 4
overview: Richard pits the Porsche Cayenne Turbo S against the British Army's freefall parachute display team.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy finds out whether the suspension on the new Citroen C6 is all it's cracked up to be.

name: Episode 6
overview: After lambasting caravans over the years, the team embarks on a caravanning holiday in Dorset.

name: Episode 7
overview: Jeremy and the Stig race two Caterham 7s around a circuit, but Jeremy needs to build his car first.

name: Episode 8
overview: The lads end the series in style - behind the wheels of their very own white vans.

name: Episode 1
overview: They make petrol, judge the Jaguar XKR, and talk to Jamie Oliver. Richard shows the disturbing footage of his jet car accident.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy makes a public information film, James drives quickly and all three get thrown off a golf course in a riot of bad driving and appalling trousers. Hugh Grant is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: USA Special
overview: Motoring magazine with Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond, who think that a fly-drive holiday to Florida would be more fun if you bought a car instead of renting one.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy tries out a Porsche 911 Turbo, and Richard and James try to build a space shuttle out of a Reliant Robin.

name: Episode 5
overview: They go rural as they harness a group of high-tech tractors and attempt to grow their own petrol.

name: Episode 6
overview: This week they build their own stretch limos out of the most improbable cars.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James drive three lightweight supercars across Europe in search of the best roads. Dame Helen Mirren is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy, James and Richard again attempt to build amphibious cars. The Audi R8 is on the Top Gear track and Jools Holland is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy drives the smallest car ever made, Richard races a Bugatti Veyron against the RAF's Typhoon jet fighter, and the Ferrari 599 rips up the Top Gear track.

name: Botswana Special
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James are challenged to each drive a second-hand car across the wilds of Botswana, facing obstacles such as salt pans, rivers and animals.

name: Episode 5
overview: The guys race across London: Jeremy in a speedboat, James in a car, Richard on his push bike and the Stig on public transport. The star in a reasonably priced car is Simon Cowell.

name: Episode 6
overview: Jeremy goes mad in two fast estates, Richard invents motorhome racing and James pits an Alfa Romeo against a man who crosses the Humber estuary without using a bridge. The Stig attacks the test track in Honda's new Civic Type R.

name: Episode 7
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James celebrate 40 years of British Leyland by reviewing three of their cars. Also, a review of the Aston Martin DBS, Jennifer Saunders as the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car, and whatever happened to the beach buggy?

name: Episode 8
overview: Motoring magazine show with James May, Richard Hammond and Jeremy Clarkson. Includes interviews with Lewis Hamilton and James Blunt, and a BMW that can drive itself.

name: Episode 9
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James enter a 24-hour endurance race in what turns out to be one of their toughest challenges to date. James tries the new Fiat 500 on the mean streets of Budapest, and the Ferrari 430 Scuderia hammers around the test track.

name: Episode 10
overview: In the last show of the series, Jeremy, James and Richard take the new BMW M3, Mercedes C63 AMG and Audi RS4 to Spain for a thorough test of performance.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James buy a bargain-priced police cruiser, while a new member of the Top Gear team shows remarkable daring.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy falls in love with the Mercedes CLK Black, Richard meets the Audi RS6 and some skiers, and the show's bravery expert is set an MG Maestro challenge.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson conducts the least appropriate power test ever, and Gavin and Stacey actors James Corden and Rob Brydon are the Stars in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy tries to drive the new Nissan GT-R across Japan faster than Richard and James can go by bullet train. Fiona Bruce and Kate Silverton are Stars in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May show off the luxury cars bought for the price of a new Ford. Meanwhile, the Nissan GT-R is given a thorough workout at Japan's legendary Fuji circuit.

name: Episode 6
overview: In the last show of the current series, Jeremy, Richard and James take on their rivals from the German equivalent of Top Gear, James drives some Japanese sportsmen in a very strange Japanese car, and Richard attacks the track in a remarkable new supercar from Gumpert.

name: Episode 1
overview: The team each buy a second-hand lorry, Jeremy tests a scary Porsche 911 on the track, and Michael Parkinson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 2
overview: The team travels to the US to take new generation muscle cars on an epic road trip from San Francisco to Utah. Will Young is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: James travels to the frozen north to find out why the Finns are such good racing drivers, and Mark Wahlberg is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 4
overview: The boys try to drive from Switzerland to north-west England on just one tank of fuel. James tackles his first-ever power test in a Pagani Zonda F Roadster.

name: Episode 5
overview: Richard Hammond celebrates the 40th birthday of the Ferrari Daytona on the Italian Riviera. Jeremy Clarkson tests the new BMW M3 saloon and the Lexus IS-F.

name: Episode 6
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May invade Greenham Common airbase to ask if Communists have ever made a good car. London mayor Boris Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 7
overview: James May travels to California to take a look at what is being hailed as the future of motoring, the hydrogen-powered Honda Clarity.

name: Episode 8
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May go to Vietnam and are told to travel the length of the country in eight days - a task the US military failed to do in ten years.

name: Episode 1
overview: In an epic London-to-Edinburgh race, Jeremy Clarkson takes the Tornado steam train, Richard Hammond is on a Vincent Black Shadow motorbike and James May drives an old Jaguar XK120.

name: Episode 2
overview: The team try to find the perfect car for 17-year-olds, negotiating insurance companies, accident repairs and a music festival car park.

name: Episode 3
overview: Clarkson, Hammond and May confront the credit crunch as they search for three sensibly priced small cars that will not make their owners miserable.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their latest and most deadly toys. Sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James seek petrolhead heaven in three rear-wheel-drive coupes and find themselves entered in a terrifying French ice race.

name: Episode 6
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James immerse themselves in the world of classic cars to find out if they really are more fun than modern machines.

name: Episode 7
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are set the task of creating a memorable TV advert for Volkswagen, but the boys quickly fall out on set.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May head to Romania in search of a fabled stretch of road. Eric Bana is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James build an electric car, the Corvette ZR1 goes up against the Audi R8 V10, and actor Michael Sheen is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond nominate the greatest car company, James May tests a congestion-busting caravan plan and DJ Chris Evans is in the studio.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson travels to Belfast to test the Renaultsport Twingo 133. Meanwhile, Richard Hammond invents a new sport - airport vehicle racing.

name: Episode 5
overview: The team fills an art gallery with motoring-related works to try to prove cars are more popular than traditional art. Also Jeremy test-drives the Noble M600.

name: Bolivia Special
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to drive from the heart of Bolivia to the coast of Chile in three 4x4s bought from the local small ads.

name: Episode 7
overview: Jeremy Clarkson makes a film about the BMW X6, James May drives a Vauxhall Insignia VXR and Richard Hammond tests a Lexus supercar.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson asks why there aren't more three-wheeled cars in the world, while James May attempts to drive a Toyota Hilux up an active volcano in Iceland.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to find a trio of cars which are equally at home on the racetrack as they are transporting a family to the shops.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to find the world's greatest four-seat supercar. Plus Richard is on the test track in two very different muscle cars.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to build cool and interesting motorhomes before setting off for a bank holiday weekend in the West Country.

name: Episode 5
overview: Tom Cruise and Cameron Diaz set lap times, Richard races Swedish snowmobilers and James May attempts to break his personal speed record in a Bugatti Veyron.

name: Episode 6
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May buy old British roadsters from Jensen-Healey, Lotus and TVR before visiting the remnants of the UK's sports car industry.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson subjects the Skoda Yeti to a remarkably thorough road test, while James May makes a rare visit to the test track in the insane Aerial Atom V8.

name: Episode 2
overview: The Top Gear presenters go head-to-head against their opposite numbers from the Australian Top Gear in a car-based version of The Ashes.

name: Episode 3
overview: The team head to Albania to test a trio of super luxurious cars including the Rolls-Royce Ghost, the Bentley Mulsanne and the Mercedes S65 AMG.

name: Episode 4
overview:
Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to buy second-hand convertibles and discover some of the unusual pitfalls to watch out for when picking up a used car bargain.

Jeremy is on the test track in the powerful Pagani Zonda R, whilst Simon Pegg is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 5
overview: The team sets out to end the chaos that snowfall brings to Britain every year, using a combine harvester, some basic engineering skills and a quick trip to Norway.

name: Episode 6
overview: Jeremy races the Jaguar XJ against something even bigger and more powerful: the earth's rotation. Richard tests 1980s icons the Porsche 959 and Ferrari F40.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the 50th birthday of the Jaguar E-type by driving a modern half-a-million pound interpretation of this classic sports car.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Italy to take part in a series of tricky challenges as they attempt to find the world's best hot hatchback.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond find some second-hand bargains for the same price as the Nissan Pixo. Sebastian Vettel is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May replace the conventional train's carriages and locomotive with a series of caravans attached to a specially modified car.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James are challenged to knock down a row of derelict houses in less time than it takes a team of demolition experts to do the same job.

name: Episode 6
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May go on a trip to the British seaside in two new electric cars, whilst Richard Hammond meets the world's most extraordinary rally team.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May head to Italy for a supercar shootout featuring the Lamborghini Aventador, the McLaren MP4-12C and the Noble M600.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May travel to Beijing for a look at China's ever-expanding car industry and a violent encounter with the Stig's Chinese cousin.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond are on location with the Sweeney movie as they take charge of shooting the film's climactic car chase.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on an ambitious project to build mobility scooters that can tackle the wilds of the British countryside.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May pay tribute to deceased Swedish car maker Saab. Richard Hammond races a rocket-powered flying man against a rally-spec Skoda.

name: Episode 6
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May hit Donington in three stripped-out track cars. And Jeremy recreates the Battle of Britain with an aero-engined Bentley.

name: Episode 7
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May set out to discover if you can go motor racing for less than it costs to play golf. Slash and F1 driver Kimi Raikkonen are guests.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson attempts to build a car that is even smaller than the famous Peel P50 and tests it out on the streets of London.

name: Episode 2
overview: The team is on a road trip from Las Vegas to Los Angeles and on to Palm Springs, taking in racing circuits, airborne attacks and a race against the police.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May engage in an epic race from Wembley to the San Siro stadium in Milan.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May play a rather unusual game of rugby and Richard Hammond goes to Mexico for a nervous road test.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and Richard Hammond tackle the problems of an ageing population and the increasing complexity of modern cars by designing a vehicle exclusively for the elderly.

name: Africa Special (1)
overview: In the first of a two-part Top Gear special, Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are in Africa with a simple mission - to find the definitive source of the Nile.

name: Africa Special (2)
overview: Having survived insane traffic, lethal mudslides and dubious map reading, the trio reach the climax of their quest to find the definitive source of the Nile.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May race a car against a boat in New Zealand. In the UK, Richard Hammond attempts to find a hot hatch hero from a trio of newcomers.

name: Episode 2
overview: Featuring a motorsport-powered search for the world's best taxi and a tribute to BBC Television Centre. Film director Ron Howard is the star in the brand new Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May visit Spain in a McLaren 12C Spider, Ferrari 458 Spider and an Audi R8 V10 Spyder.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May bring relief to flood-stricken areas of Britain by building a car that can turn into a hovercraft.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May investigate an increasingly popular type of tall hatchback often referred to as a 'crossover'.

name: Episode 6
overview: Inspired by the Jaguar F-type, Range Rover Sport and New Bus for London, the team celebrates all that is great about Britain's motor industry.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to prove that the hot hatchbacks of their youth were better than their modern equivalents.

name: Episode 2
overview: A jam-packed show featuring Italian sports cars, military manoeuvres and a trip to Belgium. Actor Tom Hiddleston is out on the track as the Star in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May demonstrate their love for compact, small-engined cars with a trip to Ukraine.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson celebrates the return of the handcrafted, coach-built car with a trip to Italy to drive the Alfa Romeo-based Touring Disco Volante.

name: Episode 5
overview: Richard Hammond is in Abu Dhabi to drive a new hybrid sports car from Porsche. Jeremy Clarkson judges a hot hatchback showdown between a Volkswagen Golf GTI and the BMW M135i.

name: Burma Special (1)
overview: This special edition of the motoring show sees Jeremy, Richard and James drive across Burma in three suspect lorries, on a mission to build a bridge over the River Kwai.

name: Burma Special (2)
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James head for the volatile Shan state and towards their final challenge - to build a bridge over the River Kwai.

name: Episode 1
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May race each other across St Petersburg. May drives, Hammond cycles and Clarkson pilots a hovercraft.

name: Episode 2
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a grand road trip across Australia's Northern Territory, each driving a fast and luxurious GT.

name: Episode 3
overview: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May attempt to improve and enhance the ambulance. F1 driver Daniel Ricciardo is the star in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 4
overview: Jeremy Clarkson tests the new BMW M3 and Richard Hammond marks the imminent demise of the legendary Land Rover Defender.

name: Episode 5
overview: Jeremy Clarkson and James May encounter artillery shells, corsets and classic hot hatchbacks as they take a nostalgic look at Peugeot.

name: Episode 6
overview: Richard Hammond is dropped into remote Canada to test a watch with a built-in emergency beacon. Gillian Anderson tries her hand in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 7
overview: James May is taken outside his comfort zone - World Rallycross racing in a 600-horsepower hatchback. Nicholas Hoult tries his hand in the Reasonably Priced Car.

name: Episode 8
overview: Jeremy, Richard and James immerse themselves in the lifestyle of the traditional classic car enthusiast and later try to become lifestyle leisure enthusiasts.

name: Episode 1
overview: Chris Evans takes the Dodge Viper ACR to Nevada to battle Sabine Schmitz in a Chevrolet Corvette Z06. Matt LeBlanc tackles wildest Morocco in Somerset's finest off-roader.

name: Episode 2
overview: Chris Evans, Matt LeBlanc and Eddie Jordan take music stars Sharleen Spiteri, Seasick Steve and Tinie Tempah on the ultimate SUV test. Damian Lewis is the Star in a Rallycross Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Chris Evans struggles to keep his lunch down as he rides in an Audi R8. Rory Reid tests the new Ford Focus RS and Matt LeBlanc tours London in Ken Block's fearsome 'Hoonicorn'.

name: Episode 4
overview: Chris Evans, Matt LeBlanc and Sabine Schmitz buy cheap second-hand cars and head for Venice. The aim? To prove road travel can be more luxurious than taking the train.

name: Episode 5
overview: This week, Chris Harris is with the new BMW M2 and Rory Reid retraces a historic journey against the clock, delivering the Jaguar F-type SVR to the Geneva Motor Show.

name: Episode 6
overview: Matt LeBlanc gets his hands on the Porsche 911 R, Chris Harris puts Honda's reimagined NSX through its paces and Chris Evans finds old-fashioned charm in the new MGB Roadster.

name: Episode 1
overview: Matt LeBlanc, Chris Harris and Rory Reid race across Kazakhstan in three high-mileage cars: a Mercedes saloon, a Volvo estate and a London taxi. James McAvoy guest stars.

name: Episode 2
overview: Matt LeBlanc and Chris Harris embark on a US road trip in the latest convertible supercars from Lamborghini and Porsche. David Tennant takes a spin in the Reasonably Fast Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Matt LeBlanc's review of the Aston Martin DB11 is thwarted by Chris Harris and a very evil Mercedes. Tamsin Greig takes to the Top Gear track in the new Reasonably Fast Car.

name: Episode 4
overview: Chris Harris and the Bugatti Chiron race Matt LeBlanc across the Arabian peninsula. Rory Reid constructs a life-size arcade game and Tinie Tempah faces the Top Gear track.

name: Episode 5
overview: Matt LeBlanc is the first to test America's explosive new supercar, the Ford GT. Presenters Chris Harris and Sabine Schmitz go head to head in one of motorsport's toughest events - a buggy race across the wild Californian desert.

name: Episode 6
overview: Matt LeBlanc gets to grips with the fire-breathing Mercedes-AMG GT R supercar. Elsewhere, Chris Harris and Rory Reid journey across Cuba in a pair of second-hand sports cars, while comedian Ross Noble takes to the Top Gear track in the Reasonably Fast Car.

name: Episode 7
overview: Matt LeBlanc drives an eight-wheeled rescue vehicle from Russia, Rory Reid turns the world's ugliest car into a luxury yacht and Chris Harris reviews the new Porsche Cayman. Jamiroquai frontman Jay Kay is the studio guest.

name: Episode 1
overview: The series kicks off with Matt LeBlanc, Chris Harris and Rory Reid taking a motorsports road trip across America's Wild West, in the latest V8 sports cars from McLaren, Jaguar and Ford. Rob Brydon tackles the Top Gear track in the Reasonably Fast Car.

name: Episode 2
overview: Matt LeBlanc and Chris Harris head into the Californian woods to test off-road toys and hunt for Big Foot. Rory Reid invents the sport of reverse camera racing, while Chris tests the new McLaren 720S. Lee Mack is this week's studio guest.

name: Episode 3
overview: There is a Japanese flavour to the show as Matt LeBlanc and Chris Harris buy second-hand sports cars at auction for a big road trip across Honshu, while Rory has one night to discover the weirdest car culture Tokyo has to offer. Out on track, Chris compares the Honda Civic Type R and Lexus LC500.

name: Episode 4
overview: Matt LeBlanc tests the world's fastest-accelerating car, with a little help from Nasa. Chris Harris pens a heartfelt love letter to the Citroen 2CV, while Rory Reid samples some exciting cars from South Korea. Dara O Briain and Ed Byrne are the studio guests.

name: Episode 5
overview: Matt LeBlanc and Chris Harris attempt to speed up British roads - and farming. Matt also tests out the blistering Ferrari 812 Superfast, while Rory Reid heads to America to tackle a muscle car banned in Europe. Vicky McClure is the studio guest.

name: Episode 6
overview: Matt LeBlanc, Chris Harris and Rory Reid test the latest SUVs through the medium of horse trialling. Chris also gets an explosive drive in the new Alpine A110, while Jason Manford is the studio guest.

name: Episode 1
overview: Matt LeBlanc and Chris Harris head to Norway in the latest estate cars from Porsche and Ferrari, while Rory Reid and Sabine Schmitz aim to create Britain’s newest mountain with the help of a tiny Suzuki. James Marsden is the studio guest.

name: Episode 2
overview: Matt LeBlanc and Chris Harris take a road trip across Sri Lanka in a pair of tiny tuk-tuks. Back at the track, Chris tests out the latest super-saloons from BMW and Mercedes, while Professor Green sets a lap in the Reasonably Fast Car.

name: Episode 3
overview: Matt LeBlanc and Chris Harris are both tasked with reviewing the new Bentley Continental GT, while Rory Reid tests the new Renault Megane hot hatch. Out on track, Chris attempts to tame the scariest Porsche 911 of all time. Gregory Porter is the studio guest.

name: Episode 4
overview: Matt LeBlanc, Chris Harris and Rory Reid buy used luxury limousines from Rolls-Royce, Bentley and Mercedes for the price of Britain's cheapest new car, and test them out in a six-hour endurance race at Silverstone. Matt Baker is the studio guest.

name: Episode 5
overview: Matt LeBlanc samples the new Aston Martin V8 Vantage on track, while Rory Reid tests the self-proclaimed ‘best car in the world’, the Rolls-Royce Phantom. Chris Harris explains why a Ford Fiesta is better than a Lamborghini. Stephen Mangan is the studio guest.

name: Episode 1
overview: In their first adventure together, Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the searing heat of Ethiopia in their first cars. Back at the track, Chris puts the Ferrari 488 Pista and McLaren 600LT through their paces.

name: Episode 2
overview: It is an electric car special as Chris, Freddie and Paddy each attempt to build their own battery-powered sports car on a budget. Chris tests the Tesla Model 3 on track, while Danny Boyle and Himesh Patel are the guest stars.

name: Episode 3
overview: Chris, Freddie and Paddy set out to turn an old hearse into the ultimate family car. On the track, Chris gets to grips with the lightweight Dallara Stradale, while Zara and Mike Tindall go head to head in the Reasonably Fast Car.

name: Episode 4
overview: Paddy and Freddie head to Borneo for a jungle adventure with the Gurkhas in a pair of very rare, very cheap old cars. Chris Harris tries out the world's most luxurious SUV, the Rolls-Royce Cullinan, while Bob Mortimer is the studio guest.

name: Episode 5
overview: Freddie Flintoff heads to Iceland to compete in the world's maddest motorsport, while Paddy McGuinness gets to grips with the new Toyota Supra. Chris Harris investigates the story of the Lotus 79, the most beautiful F1 car of all time.

name: Episode 1
overview: Paddy, Freddie and Chris head on a British summer holiday in super-cheap second-hand convertibles. Chris tests the new Ariel Atom on track, and Freddie attempts to bungee-jump an old Rover off a dam.

name: Episode 2
overview: Paddy reveals his ultimate cheap, luxurious off-roader: the homebuilt Dirty Rascal. Chris races the £2.5m McLaren Speedtail against the RAF’s newest fighter jet. Romesh Ranganathan is the studio guest.

name: Episode 3
overview: Paddy, Freddie and Chris are sent on a high-altitude mountain expedition across Peru in second-hand American cars. Back at the track, Chris tests out the latest electric car from Porsche.

name: Episode 4
overview:
Freddie and Chris tackle the toughest off-road race on the planet: the brutal Baja 1000. Out on the track, there is a showdown between a Renault hot hatch, a Porsche sports car - and an old Lamborghini.

Laurence and Emilia Fox are the studio guests.

name: Episode 5
overview:
Paddy, Freddie and Chris take the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston and Porsche on a Yorkshire road trip.

On the track, Chris tests out Volkswagen’s fearsome ID-R electric racer.

YouTube sensation KSI is the studio guest.

name: Episode 6
overview: Paddy, Freddie and Chris find out which is the fastest of the emergency services: fire, police or ambulance. Chris celebrates legendary rally driver Colin McRae, while Paddy tests the new BMW M8. Tom Allen is the studio guest.

name: Episode 1
overview: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness return with a new drive-in studio. They also visit Paddy’s home town of Bolton to test three new company cars by spending 24 hours in them - with no getting out. And Chris heads to Italy to test the blistering Ferrari SF90 supercar.

name: Episode 2
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy tackle a monstrous wall of death in some very cheap insurance write-offs. The trio also take a Yorkshire road trip in classic supercars, and Freddie attempts to hit 200mph in an old Jaguar.

name: Episode 3
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy head to Cyprus on a budget rental car adventure, and sample the exciting new sport of car water-skiing. Back at the test track, Chris and Paddy sample the latest family cars from Audi and Lamborghini.

name: Episode 4
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy are in Wales, taking a glamorous road trip in the newest grand tourers from Bentley, Aston Martin and McLaren, before taking on their German rivals and Formula One legend Heinz-Harald Frentzen. Plus a tribute to the late, great Sir Stirling Moss.

name: Episode 5
overview: Paddy introduces Chris and Freddie to his latest creation: an electric, all-terrain ice cream van for the 21st century. They also test the battery-powered Honda E, Vauxhall Corsa-e and Mini Electric in a race around a deserted Alton Towers.

name: Episode 1
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy head to the Lake District for an emotional reunion with their own dads' old cars. Elsewhere, Chris heads out on the track to test Lamborghini's most powerful car ever, the hybrid Sian.

name: Episode 2
overview: The team try out some of James Bond’s greatest cars, including the legendary Aston Martin DB5. Freddie and an Extreme E race car take on a man with a jet pack, while Chris and Paddy test a new Ferrari against a very old Alfa.

name: Episode 3
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy head out on an icy expedition across the wild Scottish Highlands in the new Land Rover Defender, Mercedes G-Wagen and Ariel Nomad. Which will prove itself the ultimate off-roader?

name: Episode 4
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy confront the male mid-life crisis by buying the perfect sports cars for ‘gentlemen of a certain age’. Back at the track, Chris tests out the raucous new Toyota GR Yaris hot hatch.

name: Episode 1
overview: Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness head to the British Grand Prix for a showdown with a trio of F1’s finest drivers. Elsewhere, Paddy pays tribute to the legendary Eddie Kidd OBE.

name: Episode 2
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy set out to revolutionise the world of caravanning, with the help of some new electric cars and some very small caravans. Out on track, Chris reviews the extravagant new Lamborghini Huracan STO.

name: Episode 3
overview: The team set out on an expedition across Iceland, with the help of some temperamental old British cars. Back in the UK, Freddie gets his hands on America’s latest sports car, the new Chevrolet Corvette.

name: Episode 4
overview: Paddy reviews the very rare, very expensive Aston Martin Victor, while Freddie sets out to become a proper, qualified racing driver. Chris investigates the stranger-than-fiction story of the time-travelling DMC DeLorean.

name: Episode 5
overview: Freddie, Chris and Paddy set out to find interesting old cars for new drivers, then put them to the test on a road trip through Devon and Cornwall. Chris drives the original supercar, Lamborghini’s legendary Miura.

name: Episode 1
